items.correct: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs — balancing economic, social and environmental goals.
items.misconception: Sustainable development means stopping all economic growth.
items.random_wrong: Sustainable development means using only solar power.
items.why: Students treat sustainability as anti-development. The Brundtland definition is about balance and intergenerational equity — not zero growth or one specific technology.

items.q: Why do tropical cyclones only form over warm tropical oceans?
items.correct: They need sea surface temperatures above ~27 degrees C to a depth of 50m to provide energy through evaporation, plus enough Coriolis effect (away from the equator) to spin.
items.misconception: Hurricanes form wherever there are strong winds over the sea.
items.random_wrong: Hurricanes form when two cold fronts collide above the ocean.
items.why: Wind doesn't create hurricanes — warm ocean does. They draw energy from latent heat of evaporating seawater; that's why they weaken rapidly over land or cool water.

items.q: What happens during an El Nino event?
items.correct: Trade winds weaken, warm water that normally pools in the western Pacific spreads east, disrupting weather patterns globally — drought in Australia, floods in South America.
items.misconception: El Nino is when the ocean gets colder near Peru.
items.random_wrong: El Nino is the name of a specific hurricane that hit Mexico.
items.why: El Nino brings WARM water to Peru — the cold-water state is La Nina. Students mix up the two phases; the name 'the Christ child' refers to warm currents at Christmas.
Correct answer: 090°. “045°” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which direction is opposite to North?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: South. “East” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many points on the 8-point compass?
items.why: The real figure is 8. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
